summary: Superb sound‑board capture of a classic 1993 Oregon show highlighted by an epic Standing On The Moon and crystal‑clear China > Rider
review: The SBD source is crisp, clean and exceptionally balanced, delivering every nuance from Jerry’s new axe to the soaring keys on Promised Land. Musically the night shines with a towering Standing On The Moon, a monster China > Rider, and a tight first‑set that, while short, feeds into a second set that many reviewers call one of the best of the ’90s.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews, most rating 4‑5 stars, consistently praising both the recording fidelity and the performance; only a few mixed comments do not outweigh the strong consensus.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean SBD with superb clarity; occasional extreme panning noted but overall superb mix and balance across band and audience ambience.

poor_songs: Here Comes Sunshine (HCS) – muddled in a few accounts
Bird Song – disappointing to one reviewer
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is short but tight, serving as a launchpad for a second‑set that builds to an unforgettable Standing On The Moon climax; the show’s pacing keeps energy high despite occasional early‑set brevity.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for his new guitar tone, incendiary solos on SOTM and Rider, and vocal delivery (‘be with you’ lyric).
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass work highlighted as ‘crushing’ the Rider groove.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals back after surgery; guest harmonica (Huey Lewis) noted but not essential.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboard work described as ‘awesome’, especially on Promised Land and the China > Rider jam.
band_member_comments.Drums: Vince/Mickey interplay in Space praised; drums drive noted as tight throughout.
